defenseDefense News · Jun 18
Ukraine hits Moscow refinery in major drone attack on Russian capital
AI Insight
Monitor jet fuel and titanium supply chain pricing—Russian refinery disruptions accelerate aerospace material cost volatility; lock in supplier contracts now.
Ukrainian drones hit the Russian capital’s oil refinery for the second time this week.
defenseDoD News · Jun 18
War Department Review to Ensure 'NATO 3.0' Becomes Europe-Led Defense Alliance
AI Insight

defenseDefense News · Jun 18
Apache going down near Oman a sign of air combat evolution, analysts warn
AI Insight
Position now to supply drone-detection, counter-UAS components, or Apache survivability subsystems—DoD will accelerate procurement in this space.
Experts say the U.S. Army Apache that went down near Oman offers a snapshot into how air assets must now contend with cheaper, rapidly evolving drones.
defenseDefense News · Jun 18
Germany moves two ships to Djibouti, eyeing multinational Hormuz mission this summer
AI Insight
Monitor Hormuz coalition formation for allied naval logistics/sustainment contracts; register on SAM.gov under relevant NAICS codes for foreign military sales support now.
Germany is among a number of countries that have offered help in securing the shipping lane, but only if an Iran ceasefire deal turns out to be lasting.
defenseDefense News · Jun 18

AI Insight
Track NATO readiness contract opportunities as European force restructuring drives demand for rapid-deployment parts, MRO services, and logistics support.

Adopt AI for low-stakes internal tasks (quoting, scheduling, documentation) now, but keep certified experts owning ITAR-controlled and quality-critical processes to satisfy auditors.

defenseDefense News · Jun 17

AI Insight
Review your ITAR-controlled parts and customer end-use certificates now—any U.S.-Iran nuclear deal shifts export control enforcement priorities and supply chain scrutiny overnight.

Level 2 — Advanced
110 practices · triggered by: DFARS 252.204-7012 · DFARS 252.204-7019 · DFARS 252.204-7020 · DFARS 252.204-7021

Protects CUI (Controlled Unclassified Information). Aligned with NIST SP 800-171. Triennial third-party assessment for prioritized contracts.

Implementation checklist
  • —Develop a System Security Plan (SSP) covering all 110 NIST 800-171 controls
  • —Submit SPRS score in the Supplier Performance Risk System before contract award
  • —Identify and segment all CUI flows through your network
  • —Multi-factor authentication for privileged + remote access
  • —FIPS 140-2 validated cryptography for CUI in transit + at rest
  • —Incident response plan with 72-hour DoD reporting capability
  • —Engage a C3PAO for triennial assessment if your contract requires it
  • —Annual affirmation by senior official
C3PAO Directory: cyberab.org/Catalog/C3PAOs — assessment partners authorized for Level 2 third-party assessments.
